data.title: Robinhood rolls out public blockchain as it expands deeper into crypto
data.description: Robinhood (HOOD) launched the public mainnet for its Layer 2 Robinhood Chain, a network built on Arbitrum (ARB), as it pushes further into tokenized assets and decentralized finance, according to CoinDesk. The launch makes Robinhood’s tokenized stock products fully live, with Stock Tokens available via Robinhood Wallet in more than 120 countries, and adds Robinhood Earn, a decentralized lending product offering an estimated 7% yield on USDG. Robinhood also said it is expanding perpetual futures trading in Europe, plans to launch crypto trading in the U.K., and is now available in Canada after acquiring WonderFi.

data.title: FuelCell Energy Jumps Nearly 70% in June on AI Data Center Power Pivot
data.description:
FuelCell Energy (FCEL) shares surged nearly 70% in June and recently traded around $36.25 as the company pivots toward supplying power for AI data centers. According to BeInCrypto, FCEL gained 70% across June, with the past five trading days up 79%, and was added to the Russell 3000 as of June 26.

The company said over 80% of its commercial pipeline is tied to data centers, with the pipeline up 275% year-over-year. In June, FCEL announced a Fit Energy agreement to supply up to 380 MW, including a deposit-backed 30 MW initial order starting in late 2026, and cited EXIM approval of a $49 million package for South Korea expansion.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Shanghai Gold Futures Rise 1.23% in Overnight Trading
data.description:
Shanghai gold and silver futures rose in overnight trading, while SC crude oil futures fell.

According to Jin10, as of 2:30 (UTC+8), the most-traded Shanghai gold futures contract closed up 1.23% at 888 yuan per gram, and the most-traded Shanghai silver futures contract ended up 1.44% at 14,582 yuan per kilogram.

The most-traded SC crude oil futures contract closed down 3.43% at 445 yuan per barrel.

data.title: U.S. Crude Oil Futures Settle Down 1.32% at $68.58 a Barrel
data.description: U.S. crude oil futures settled at $68.58 a barrel, down $0.92, or 1.32%. According to Jin10, the settlement price marked a decline on the day.

data.title: Arabica Coffee Extends Rally as Funds Turn More Bullish
data.description: Arabica coffee futures rose for a third straight session in New York as investors were seen turning increasingly bullish amid uncertainty over production in top exporter Brazil, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Reform UK Removes Proposed Cryptoassets Bill After Reported Undeclared £5 Million Donation
data.description:
Reform UK has removed its proposed Cryptoassets and Digital Finance Bill from its website following controversy linked to a reported £5 million donation from Christopher Harborne to Nigel Farage.

According to NS3.AI, The Nerve reported that the bill disappeared from Reform UK’s website on May 30 this year, although the PDF version can still be found online.

The Guardian reported that Harborne holds a 12% stake in Tether and said the donation was not declared on the parliamentary register of interests.

data.title: Getty Images M&A Bond Jumps 14 Cents to Near Par After Shutterstock Deal Termination Plan
data.description: A bond issued by Getty Images Holdings Inc. to fund its acquisition of Shutterstock Inc. rose 14 cents on Wednesday to near par after the company said it plans to terminate the deal, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: WORLD CUP | Kane scores twice as England rallies past DR Congo 2-1
data.description: Harry Kane scored in the 75th minute and again 10 minutes later as England came from behind to beat DR Congo 2-1 in Atlanta, according to Yahoo Sports. Brian Cipenga put DR Congo ahead in the seventh minute, and Yoane Wissa hit the post in the 42nd as Congo threatened to extend its lead. Kane was denied a penalty after a collision with goalkeeper Lionel Mpasi, but later headed in the equalizer and then fired the winner into the top right corner. England will next face Mexico in Mexico City.

data.title: US Crude Grades Lose Iran War Premium as Energy Crisis Eases
data.description: Key US oil grades have fallen back to trading at a discount as crude continues to flow through the Strait of Hormuz and a recent surge in demand for US exports has faded, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: CAS Space Updates STAR Market IPO Filing, Discloses 2025 Results; LandSpace Also Refreshes Prospectus
data.description:
CAS Space Technology Co. updated its STAR Market IPO prospectus on the Shanghai Stock Exchange website on June 29 and disclosed full-year 2025 financials, while private rocket peer LandSpace also updated its STAR Market filing the same day, according to Jiemian News.

Exchange information shows CAS Space’s IPO application was accepted on March 31, 2026, and it received review inquiries on April 15, 2026; its review status remains “in inquiry.” CAS Space reported revenue of RMB 77.72 million in 2023, RMB 244 million in 2024 and RMB 277 million in 2025, with rocket launch services contributing 97.5% of 2025 revenue. The company posted a net loss attributable to shareholders of RMB 1.119 billion in 2025 and nearly RMB 2.5 billion in cumulative net losses over 2023-2025; operating cash flow was negative for a third straight year at RMB -865 million in 2025.

CAS Space said it plans to raise RMB 4.18 billion, with 52.21% earmarked for R&D of a large reusable launch vehicle, and management forecasts profitability in 2029 under optimistic and base-case assumptions, or 2030 under a conservative case.

data.title: AI TRENDS | WSJ: Prototype Device to Use xAI-Integrated Operating System and Qualcomm Snapdragon Chipset
data.description:
A prototype device is set to run a proprietary operating system that integrates artificial intelligence technology from SpaceX’s xAI, according to people familiar with the matter.

According to Jin10, The Wall Street Journal reported that the prototype has a sleek design and is thinner than the iPhone.

The report said the device will use a Qualcomm Snapdragon chipset.

data.title: STOCKS | Qualcomm Shares Rise 1.6% in Brief Move Higher
data.description: Qualcomm shares rose 1.6% in a brief upward move. According to Jin10, the stock saw a short-term lift in its price.

data.title: Iran Says U.S. Violated Memorandum Commitments, Plans Oversight Mechanism Review
data.description:
Iran said the United States violated commitments under a U.S.-Iran memorandum of understanding and announced plans to set up an oversight group to review alleged breaches.

According to Jin10, Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister and head of Iran’s technical negotiating delegation Gharibabadi said Iran raised and reviewed what it described as a U.S. violation of Article 1 of the memorandum, which he said concerned a commitment to end the fighting in Lebanon.

Gharibabadi said the Iranian delegation made the remarks during a trilateral meeting involving Iran, Qatar, and Pakistan held in Doha on July 1. He added that the talks also discussed reports about the United States strengthening regional military equipment and troop deployments, as well as what he described as threatening and interventionist statements by U.S. officials.

He said Iran emphasized during the meeting that the commitments in the memorandum should be treated as an integrated package and should not be viewed in isolation.

Gharibabadi said Iran would form a supervisory team and use direct communication channels to report on and review alleged violations related to the memorandum.

data.title: Venga Receives MiCA Authorization From Spain’s Securities Regulator to Operate Across the EU
data.description:
Spain’s National Securities Market Commission has authorized cryptocurrency platform Venga to operate under the EU’s Markets in Crypto-Assets Regulation (MiCA), making it one of a small number of MiCA-approved entities in Spain.

According to Odaily, MiCA’s transition period took effect on July 1, requiring digital asset companies that previously operated under national rules to obtain formal approval under the new regime or stop providing regulated services in the EU.

The authorization allows Venga to offer services across the EU’s market of 27 member states under a single regulatory framework.

Venga co-founder and CEO Michael Stroev said the MiCA review covers areas including ownership structure, corporate governance, management suitability, risk frameworks, and product infrastructure.

The report said more than 3,000 crypto companies in the EU previously held local virtual asset service provider registrations, while about 240 companies have now received full MiCA authorization. It added that fewer than 15 companies in Spain have been approved.

data.title: Finance App Network Maker Plaid Said to Consider US IPO
data.description: Plaid Inc., which provides a network that helps fintech apps connect to customers’ financial accounts, is considering a US initial public offering, according to people familiar with the matter, Bloomberg reported.

data.title: Florida's HB 505 Sets Daily Caps and Refund Rules for Virtual Currency Kiosks
data.description:
Florida's HB 505, now Chapter 2026-178, establishes a regulatory framework for virtual currency kiosks, including fraud warnings, receipts, daily transaction caps, registration filings, and a conditional right to a refund.

According to NS3.AI, most provisions of the act take effect Jan. 1, 2027.

The law sets daily caps of $2,000 for new customers and $10,000 for existing customers.

data.title: BlackRock’s Tseng to Exit as CEO of Troubled Private Credit Fund
data.description: The head of BlackRock Inc.’s beleaguered private credit fund is in the process of leaving the firm, following months of losses on soured loans and revelations of a US regulatory probe into the unit’s valuation practices, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Miles Guo Sentenced to 30 Years in $1 Billion Fraud Scheme Involving Fake Himalaya Coin
data.description:
Miles Guo was sentenced to 30 years in prison for a fraud scheme that raised more than $1 billion from investors. According to NS3.AI, Guo was also ordered to forfeit $889 million.

The report said part of the scheme involved a fake cryptocurrency called Himalaya Coin.

data.title: STOCKS | European Stocks Slip as Traders Weigh ECB Forum Signals
data.description: European stocks ended lower but well off session lows on Wednesday after policymakers at the European Central Bank’s annual forum signaled they will not rush to raise interest rates, according to Bloomberg.

data.title: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Says Doha Talks Have Ended
data.description: Iran’s deputy foreign minister said Iran’s delegation has concluded talks in Doha. According to Jin10, the statement was reported by the Islamic Republic News Agency (IRNA).
